title: senecio adrianicus
intro: Senecio adrianicus, also known as White Horehound, is a plant species native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe, characterized by its white flowers and a bitter taste. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an expectorant and antispasmodic, helping to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The health benefits of Senecio adrianicus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be found in combination with other herbs in cough medicines and respiratory remedies.
